title: Unstable Shelf Concordance
hypothesis: Unstable Shelf Concordance tests whether archive loss can be felt through irreversible filing decisions instead of decorative particles.
dataModel: variable shelf slots with conflicting accession numbers and movable separators; mutation:witness-merge
recordCountStrategy: seeded variable 11-25 records with no constant-size record array
interactionModel: drag separators to split shelves; clicked records inherit new shelf ids and persist conflicts; mutation mode witness-merge
stateModel: localStorage stores recovered, locked, and misfiled record ids for this draft id
visualLanguage: thin shelf rulers, harsh registration marks, damaged accession text
expectedStructuralChange: Interaction must change at least one record position, class, label, count, and persisted state.
